steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure and Purpose question. The correct answer names the overall purpose — what the author is trying to do with the entire passage, not just one detail. Every element of the text should connect to that purpose.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To show how much Max wants to visit La Reina: The passage opens with Max begging Papa to take him, imagines being a hero among friends for crossing the haunted gates, and ends with Max hoping Papa will agree this summer. Every sentence builds his longing for La Reina. ✓
  • Choice B — To portray how proud Max's father is of Max: Papa is mentioned only as someone whose permission Max is seeking. The passage says nothing about Papa being proud. ✗
  • Choice C — To criticize Max for disliking summer: Max does not dislike summer — he hopes this summer Papa will finally take him. The tone is hopeful, not critical. ✗
  • Choice D — To explain why Max doesn't want to grow up yet: The passage never suggests Max resists growing up. His wish to visit La Reina is about adventure, not about avoiding adulthood. ✗
That is the correct answer.
